paper_title: Assessing the Quality of Flux Chamber Measurements

methods.description: This method describes the installation of flux chambers to measure ecosystem fluxes, addressing potential biases and disturbances caused by the installation process.

steps.description: Install 280 collars in the ground before starting the experiment to prevent leaking of air.
steps.searchable_summary: install collars in the ground to prevent air leakage

steps.description: Cut a shallow slit in the ground to tightly fit in collars.
steps.searchable_summary: cut slit in ground for collar fitting

steps.description: Assess the potential disturbance effects on soil and plant roots due to collar installation.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate disturbance effects from collar installation

steps.description: Compare chamber fluxes with eddy-covariance fluxes to evaluate the impact of installation artifacts.

methods.description: This method addresses the small-scale heterogeneity in ecosystem measurements, which can affect the representativeness of chamber-based carbon flux measurements.

steps.description: Identify small-scale heterogeneity in the ecosystem, such as variations in soil temperature, soil moisture, and thaw depths.
Use more than one chamber to assess the mean and variance of surface fluxes to account for heterogeneity.
steps.searchable_summary: use multiple chambers to assess flux mean and variance
